title: fraxinus excelsior var. aurea
intro: Fraxinus excelsior var. aurea, also known as golden ash, is a variety of the ash tree (Fraxinus excelsior) characterized by its golden-yellow foliage. The bark and leaves of the golden ash contain salicin, a compound with medicinal properties that have been used to treat pain, inflammation, and fever. The health benefits of golden ash include its ability to reduce swelling, act as a natural anti-inflammatory, and provide relief from colds and flu due to its expectorant properties. Golden ash can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which are often used to soothe respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
